description: internal:rule= sibling — an EXTERNAL transition exits + re-inits the inner engine (§51.0.O / §51.0.Q). Same composite as engine-internal-transition-preserves-rt. Drive inner to .Battle (#fight), take the EXTERNAL transition out to .Title (#quit), then re-enter via the external edge Title -> .Playing (#enter). Because the composite carries no history, the outer-exit discards inner state and outer-entry re-initializes the inner engine at its initial= (.Exploring). This is the discriminating half of the internal-vs-external pair: an impl that failed to re-init the inner on external re-entry would leave .Battle here.
rationale: §51.0.O an external transition (canonical rule=) causes the composite to exit (clearing inner-engine state when history is absent) then re-enter, re-initializing the inner engine at initial= (.Exploring) per §51.0.Q lifecycle coupling.
